audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:clause + maar + clause]] and [[grammar:singular noun → plural noun in -en]]. Use [[grammar:maar]] when the second idea contrasts with the first. Many Dutch nouns form the plural by adding [[grammar:-en]].
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:clause + maar + clause]]: [[grammar:Maar]] coordinates two finite clauses and signals adversative discourse relation.; [[grammar:singular noun → plural noun in -en]]: The regular plural suffix [[grammar:-en]] may trigger ordinary spelling adjustments while preserving lexical identity.

audienceSections.normal:
#### clause + maar + clause

Use [[grammar:maar]] when the second idea contrasts with the first. Example: [[grammar:Ik verzamel de foto’s, maar Sophie maakt de poster.]]

#### singular noun → plural noun in -en

Many Dutch nouns form the plural by adding [[grammar:-en]]. Example: [[grammar:We verdelen eerst de taken.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### clause + maar + clause

[[grammar:Maar]] coordinates two finite clauses and signals adversative discourse relation. Literal example: [[grammar:Ik verzamel de foto’s, maar Sophie maakt de poster.]]

#### singular noun → plural noun in -en

The regular plural suffix [[grammar:-en]] may trigger ordinary spelling adjustments while preserving lexical identity. Literal example: [[grammar:We verdelen eerst de taken.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Maar]] connects a contrast. Regular [[grammar:plurals in -en]] are one common way to mark more than one.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Maar]] connects a contrast. Regular [[grammar:plurals in -en]] are one common way to mark more than one.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Jullie groep maakt een project over de stad.]] — Your group is making a project about the city.
- [[grammar:Ik verzamel de foto’s, maar Sophie maakt de poster.]] — I am collecting the photos, but Sophie is making the poster.
- [[grammar:Goed. We verdelen eerst de taken.]] — Good. We will divide the tasks first.
- [[grammar:Welke taak heeft Alex?]] — Which task does Alex have?
- [[grammar:Hij schrijft de teksten en kiest de foto’s.]] — He writes the texts and chooses the photos.
- [[grammar:Sophie tekent de straten, maar ik controleer de namen.]] — Sophie draws the streets, but I check the names.
- [[grammar:Wanneer presenteert de groep het project?]] — When does the group present the project?
- [[grammar:Vrijdag presenteert onze groep de poster.]] — On Friday our group presents the poster.
- [[grammar:Er zijn veel taken, maar het plan is duidelijk.]] — There are many tasks, but the plan is clear.
- [[grammar:Controleer ook de teksten van de andere groepen.]] — Also check the texts of the other groups.
- [[grammar:Dat doen we morgen.]] — We will do that tomorrow.
- [[grammar:Dan is het project op tijd klaar.]] — Then the project will be ready on time.
